item.title: Motorcyclist, boy killed in city mishaps
item.description: A child and a motorcyclist were killed in separate road accidents in the capital’s Shanir Akhra and Tejgaon areas early Monday....

item.title: Flag Hoisting Day observed
item.description: The Flag Hoisting Day was observed in Dhaka on Monday to mark the day when Bangladesh’s national flag was hoisted for the first time on the campus of the University of Dhaka in 1971....

item.title: Ex-adviser Adilur harassed at HSIA
item.description: Former housing and public works adviser Adilur Rahman Khan was allegedly harassed by the immigration officials at Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport on Monday afternoon while travelling to Nepal as an election observer....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Little magazine corner shines at fairground
item.description: With different types of activities, including discussion, poetry events and book launches, the little magazine corner shines at the Suhrawardy Udyan, which was almost visitor-less on Monday....

item.title: Protests against attack on Iran continue
item.description: Differentpoliticalparties on Monday staged demonstration in the capital protesting against the military attacks on Iran by the United States and Israel....

item.title: Environment ministry affiliates to do ‘routine’ work mostly
item.description: The seven affiliated agencies of the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change will basically do their routine administrative duties with a very limited number of new initiatives in the first 180-day work plan undertaken by the new government....

item.title: NPLs drop to Tk 5.57 lakh crore amid rescheduling before polls
item.description: Defaulted loans in Bangladesh’s banking sector fell by nearly Tk 87,000 crore within three months at the end of December 2025, mainly due to large-scale rescheduling ahead of the national election......

item.title: Exports fall for eight straight months
item.description: Bangladesh’s merchandise exports declined for the eighth consecutive month in February by a staggering 12.03 per cent year-on-year to $3.50 billion, down from $3.97 billion in February 2025, weighed down by weaker shipments of readymade garments......Foreign affairs analysis

item.title: No immediate impact on imports: commerce minister
item.description: Commerce minister Khandaker Abdul Muktadir on Monday said there will be no immediate impact on the import of essential items even though all transit through the Strait of Hormuz has been closed due to the conflict between the United States and Iran......

item.title: Gold price soars by Tk 5,424 per bhari
item.description: The price of gold in Bangladesh has increased by Tk 5,424 per bhari, with the rate of 22-carat gold set at Tk 274,104 per bhari from Monday, according to the latest update available at the time of writing this report at 7:55pm from the Bangladesh Jewellers Association......

item.title: Anti-war protests spread across world
item.description: As news circulated that Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, had been killed in US and Israeli airstrikes on Tehran, anti-war protesters gathered across the United States, including outside the White House and in New York’s Times Square to voice......

item.title: Stocks rise after plunge
item.description: Stocks on Monday reverted to the upward trajectory as investors shrugged off Middle East tensions concerning the market’s momentum and opted to regain positions in equities at attractive entry points, capitalizing on the previous session’s sharp correction......

item.title: Energy prices soar on Iran war fallout
item.description: Oil and gas prices soared, stock markets slid and the dollar firmed on Monday as the widening Iran war shook financial markets across the globe......

item.title: Iran war shakes up global shipping routes
item.description: The US-Israeli military operation against Iran and Tehran’s retaliation are disrupting global maritime traffic, including oil tanker transports......Breaking news alerts

item.title: Largest wholesale tomato market thrives in Dinajpur
item.description: The largest wholesale tomato market in northern Dinajpur is bustling with activity as more than 150 to 200 tonnes of tomatoes are supplied daily to districts across Bangladesh from Gabura Bazar in Sadar upazila......

item.title: HC order today on writ petitions challenging referendum, July charter implementation order
item.description: The High Court on Monday concluded hearings on two writ petitions filed by lawyers aligned with the Bangladesh Nationalist Party challenging the Referendum Ordinance 2025 and the July National Charter......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: The ultimate conclusion
item.description: AMERICANS should not be fooled or led astray by the corporate media or the Beltway consultant class: fact-free fear-mongering and warmongering about Iran have been among the most thoroughly bipartisan positions in Washington for many decades. For all of the turgid language within US government and media circles about rogue regimes and state sponsors of......

item.title: Semiconductor policy in domestic industry
item.description: THE global semiconductor race is often framed in terms of export performance, fabrication capacity and geopolitical rivalry. Yet experience across advanced industrial economies shows that export ambition alone cannot sustain a semiconductor ecosystem. A credible national semiconductor strategy must integrate export capability with structured domestic......

item.title: Going to war, again, for Israel
item.description: ONCE again, America is going to war for Israel. Once again, many will die for the Zionist state, including American service members. Once again, we will stumble blindly into a military fiasco. Once again, we will do the bidding of a foreign power whose interests are not our interests, but whose lobbyists have bought up our political class, including Donald Trump. Once......

item.title: Climate resilience through social enterprises
item.description: Where the rivers meet the sea and deltas stretch to the horizon, Bangladesh is learning what climate change looks like up close. In the past decade alone, more than 16 million people have been displaced by slow-onset climate shocks like riverbank erosion and salinity intrusion, according to the World Bank, while Cyclone Sitrang in 2022 caused losses estimated......

item.title: Policies yet to turn into action to stop student suicide
item.description: THE persistence of student suicide is a disgrace that demands urgent, coordinated action. When hundreds of young people die by suicide every year, the failure is not merely individual; it is systemic. An Aachol Foundation report shows that at least 403 students died by suicide in 2025, a 30 per cent increase on that in the year before. Like earlier counts based on reports, the......

item.title: Govt must address fraud in teacher certification
Trump warns of longer Iran war, Rubio points at Israel
item.description: President Donald Trump warned Monday of a longer Iran war and refused to rule out ground troops, as his top diplomat suggested the timing of the US military operation was triggered by Israel....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Drying rivers spur farming dreams, worry fishermen in Kurigram
item.description: The once-turbulent Dharla and Baromasia rivers in northern Bangladesh have quietened, leaving behind scores of new river islands or chars now turning green with crops....

item.title: IGP asks police to show zero tolerance for extortion
item.description: The newly appointed inspector general of police Md Ali Hossain Fakir on Monday gave firm message to field level police personnel to adopt ‘zero-tolerance’ policy against extortion....

item.title: Boy found dead in Rajshahi paddy field
item.description: The police on Monday recovered the body of an 11-year-old boy from a paddy field in the Beel Dharampur area under Paba upazila in Rajshahi....

item.title: 2 teens among 3 killed in road accidents
item.description: At least three people, including two teenagers, were killed in road accidents in Munshiganj, Jhenaidah, and Lakshmipur on Monday....

item.title: Govt cancels DMTCL MD’s contractual appointment
item.description: The road transport and bridges ministry has cancelled the contractual appointment of Dhaka Mass Transit Company Limited managing director Faruque Ahmed....

item.title: Seven cops injured in attack during drive in Gazipur
item.description: Seven police personnel were injured in an attack by the relatives of an abductor while attempting to arrest the alleged abductor after rescuing a kidnapped teenage girl at Sripur in Gazipur on Sunday....

item.title: Canal excavation to bring transformative change in people’s livelihoods: Anee
item.description: Water resources minister Md Shahiduddin Chowdhury Anee, on Monday said that canal excavation would bring a radical change in the livelihoods of people....

item.title: Japan provides $1.4m for Rohingya children
item.description: Japan has signed a new agreement with the United Nations Children’s Fund, providing $1.4 million to deliver life-saving support to Rohingya refugee children and families in Cox’s Bazar and Bhasan Char....

item.title: Man shot by assailants in Khulna dies
item.description: A 35-year-old man who was shot and hacked by some unidentified people in Khulna city on Sunday night died early Monday....

item.title: Rohingya youth held in Sylhet
item.description: Sylhet police detained a Rohingya youth from the city on Sunday night....

item.title: Electrician electrocuted
item.description: An electrician died after being electrocuted while working in the Shanir Akhra area under Kadamtali in Dhaka on Sunday night....

item.title: Woman crushed under train in Kushtia
item.description: A 25-YEAR-OLD woman was killed after being crushed under a train at Sadar upazila in Kushtia on Monday morning....

item.title: UK toughens asylum rules to discourage migration
item.description: The United Kingdom’s overhaul of its asylum programme takes effect on Monday, the Home Office announced, with the new rules drastically cutting protections for refugees and their children....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Nepal rivals rally on final day of election campaign
item.description: Nepal’s election campaign entered its final day on Monday, six months after deadly anti-corruption protests toppled the government, with rival leaders making a last push in a tightly contested race....

item.title: Russian strikes kill 8, hit passenger train in Ukraine
item.description: Russian strikes have killed at least eight people in Ukraine including during an attack on a civilian passenger train, Ukrainian authorities said Monday....

item.title: US assistant secretary Paul Kapur arrives today
item.description: The United States assistant secretary of state for South and Central Asian Affairs S Paul Kapur is scheduled to arrive in Dhaka today on a two-day visit aimed at strengthening strategic ties between Bangladesh and the United States....

item.title: ICT begins charge hearing against Menon, Qamrul
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Monday began hearing charges in a crimes against humanity case against detained Workers’ Party of Bangladesh president Rashed Khan Menon and Awami League leader Qamrul Islam....Top news summaries

item.title: DNC official shot at during anti-narcotics drive in Dhaka
item.description: An official of the Department of Narcotics Control was shot at by a suspected drug peddler during an anti-drug drive in the capital’s Sayedabad area on Monday....

item.title: Eight burnt in gas explosion at Mohammadpur workshop
item.description: At least eight people suffered burn injuries in an explosion reportedly originating from a gas leak at a car workshop in Dhaka’s Mohammadpur area on Monday....

item.title: Agricultural development to ensure development of country: minister
item.description: Food, fisheries and livestock and agriculture minister Mohammad Amin-ur Rashid has said that the development of agriculture would ensure overall development of the country....

item.title: Specially abled players to be salaried: Aminul
item.description: The state minister for youth and sports, Md Aminul Haque, said a plan had been taken to bring players with special needs under a specific salary structure to sustain their talent and success....Geographic Reference

item.title: Mango orchards lose land to fish ponds in Rajshahi, C’nawabganj
item.description: The land coverage of mango orchards has declined in Rajshahi and Chapainawabganj districts this year as farmers increasingly convert orchard land into fish ponds, raising concerns over changing land-use patterns in the country’s traditional mango belt....
